title: House Robber II
hints: The circular constraint makes the first and last houses adjacent, so you can never rob both. Consider breaking the circle into two linear cases.
Split the problem into two subproblems: one excluding the last house, and another excluding the first house. Solve each as a standard linear house robber problem.
For each linear subproblem, use dynamic programming where the state at index i depends on the maximum value from i-1 and i-2. Return the maximum of the two results.
